content.problemStatement:
You are given a **weighted** tree.

The distance from node xxx to node yyy is calculated as follows:


Let there be kkk edges in the shortest path from node xxx to yyy.
Let the weight of these edges, in the same **order** (from xxx to yyy), be denoted as e1,e2,‚Ä¶,eke_1, e_2, \ldots, e_ke1‚Äč,e2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,ek‚Äč.
Then, the distance from xxx to yyy is denoted as:

d(x,y)=f(e1,0)‚äēf(e2,1)‚äēf(e3,2)‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēf(ek,k‚ąí1)d(x, y) = f(e_1, 0) \oplus f(e_2, 1) \oplus f(e_3, 2) \oplus \ldots \oplus f(e_k, k-1)d(x,y)=f(e1‚Äč,0)‚äēf(e2‚Äč,1)‚äēf(e3‚Äč,2)‚äē‚Ä¶‚äēf(ek‚Äč,k‚ąí1), where ‚äē\oplus‚äē denotes the bitwise XOR operation, and

f(a,b)f(a, b)f(a,b) denotes the integer obtained after bbb left circular shifts on the integer aaa (considering its 323232 bit binary representation).

For example, f((10000000000000000000000000000001)2,3)=(00000000000000000000000000001100)2f((10000000000000000000000000000001)_2,3) = (00000000000000000000000000001100)_2f((10000000000000000000000000000001)2‚Äč,3)=(00000000000000000000000000001100)2‚Äč.
Your task is to find ‚ąĎv=1v=Nd(i,v)\sum_{v = 1}^{v = N}d(i, v)‚ąĎv=1v=N‚Äčd(i,v) for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1\le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains one integer NNN ‚ÄĒ the number of nodes.
The next N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines describe the edges. The ithi^{th}ith of these N‚ąí1N-1N‚ąí1 lines contains three space-separated integers uiu_iui‚Äč, viv_ivi‚Äč and wiw_iwi‚Äč, denoting an edge between uiu_iui‚Äč and viv_ivi‚Äč of weight wiw_iwi‚Äč.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a single line, NNN space-separated integers, ‚ąĎv=1v=Nd(i,v)\sum_{v = 1}^{v = N}d(i, v)‚ąĎv=1v=N‚Äčd(i,v) for each 1‚Č§i‚Č§N1 \le i \le N1‚Č§i‚Č§N.
constraints: It is guaranteed that the input forms a tree.
The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 10510^5105.
